seed_checks.detail: Admin user 'password_hash' is still the schema.sql placeholder 'REPLACE_WITH_HASHED_PASSWORD'. users.init_default_admin() uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so it does not overwrite the placeholder. Admin login will fail on any fresh PostgreSQL deploy using schema.sql as written.
seed_checks.recommendation: Fix: either (a) remove the admin INSERT from schema.sql and let init_default_admin() create it, or (b) change init_default_admin() to use ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET password_hash=EXCLUDED.password_hash when the existing hash equals the placeholder sentinel.

seed_checks.detail: Neither SQLite nor PostgreSQL schema includes a schema_versions / migrations table. There is no automated version drift guard. Any future schema changes must be manually tracked and applied across environments.
seed_checks.recommendation: Add a schema_migrations table (or adopt Alembic/Flyway) to track applied migrations and prevent environment drift as the schema evolves.

verdict: Schema column set is fully preserved across all 6 tables. All type changes are expected upgrades (INTEGER->BOOLEAN for flag columns, TEXT->JSONB for details, DATETIME->TIMESTAMPTZ for timestamps). PostgreSQL adds NOT NULL constraints and CHECK constraints not present in SQLite — these are hardening improvements, not regressions. CRITICAL: Admin user seed in schema.sql uses a placeholder password hash that cannot authenticate, and init_default_admin() will not overwrite it. This must be fixed before production deploy. No schema_migrations tracking exists; recommend adding one.
